TheAtwenty-second annual con-
vent of the Washington educa-
tiQ( association convened at


this afternoon at the South
htral high school. The conven.
Zion was called to order with every
foot of space available in the audi
toriHm taxed to it's utmost.


It is estimated by County Super
intendent Mcfarland this after
noon that there are at least 1900
to 1900 teachers in the city, which
number he expects will be increas-
ed to from 1000 to $500 by tomor-
row evening. Educators are said
to be looking forward to the pres
ent convention in Spokane as the
most successful and profitable in
the history of the association.


A reception will be given the
teachers and visiting educators to-
night at the Masonic temple, under
the auspices of the Women's club.
There will be music, oratory and
something good to eat.


The joint institute by the teach
ers of Spokane county and city,
Stevens and Lincoln counties clos
ed today noon. Resolutions were
adopted favoring a simpler and bet
ter plan of certifying teachers, and
also asking the legislature to main
tain the present law as to the issu-
ance of permanent licenses to
teachers holding first grade certifi-
Gates who have taught for 90
months, 36 of which were in the
state of Washington.
